flashcards.front: What is clipping in computer graphics?
flashcards.back: Clipping is the process of eliminating portions of geometric primitives (points, lines, polygons) that fall outside a specified viewing region called the viewport or window before rendering.

flashcards.front: Define viewport and window in clipping context
flashcards.back: Window is the rectangular region in world coordinates defining what to display. Viewport is the rectangular region on the output device (screen) where the window contents are mapped.

flashcards.front: What are outcodes in Cohen-Sutherland algorithm?
flashcards.back: Outcodes are 4-bit codes assigned to endpoints of a line to determine its position relative to the clipping window. Bits represent: top, bottom, right, left regions respectively.
A point (x,y) is visible if it lies within the clipping window: xw_min ≤ x ≤ xw_max and yw_min ≤ y ≤ yw_max. Otherwise, it is discarded.

flashcards.front: List the three cases in Cohen-Sutherland line clipping
flashcards.back: 1) Completely inside (both endpoints have outcode 0000) - ACCEPT. 2) Completely outside (logical AND of outcodes ≠ 0) - REJECT. 3) Partially inside - requires subdivision.

flashcards.front: What is the advantage of Liang-Barsky over Cohen-Sutherland?
flashcards.back: Liang-Barsky is more efficient as it uses parametric equations and processes each boundary as an inequality, computing intersection parameters directly without repeated subdivisions.

It clips a polygon against one clipping boundary at a time. For each edge of the input polygon, it determines whether vertices are inside/outside and adds new vertices at intersections with the clipping boundary.

flashcards.front: What is the time complexity of Cohen-Sutherland algorithm?
flashcards.back: O(1) for trivial accept/reject cases. For lines requiring clipping, it may iterate multiple times with at most one subdivision per boundary, making it practically efficient.
Liang-Barsky algorithm uses parametric form: x = x1 + u(x2-x1), y = y1 + u(y2-y1) where u∈[0,1]. It calculates entry and exit parameters for each boundary.

flashcards.front: What is Weiler-Atherton polygon clipping used for?
flashcards.back: It handles concave polygon clipping and can produce hole-containing polygons. It operates by traversing polygon vertices and inserting intersection points to create output polygon.

flashcards.front: What are the methods of text clipping?
flashcards.back: 1) All or none string clipping, 2) Individual character clipping, 3) Stroke or pixel-level clipping. Selection depends on text precision requirements.

flashcards.front: How does Cohen-Sutherland handle diagonal lines crossing corners?
flashcards.back: For diagonal lines, the algorithm finds intersection with the clipping boundary, then uses the outcode of the intersection point to determine which additional boundaries to test.

flashcards.front: What is the role of clipping in graphics pipeline?
flashcards.back: Clipping is performed after transformation but before rasterization. It ensures only visible primitives are processed, saving computational resources and preventing invalid rendering.

flashcards.front: State the parametric inequalities in Liang-Barsky algorithm
flashcards.back: p1 = -Δx, q1 = x1 - xw_min; p2 = Δx, q2 = xw_max - x1; p3 = -Δy, q3 = y1 - yw_min; p4 = Δy, q4 = yw_max - y1, where p_i=0 tests parallel edges.

flashcards.front: Why is clipping necessary before rasterization?
flashcards.back: Rasterization converts primitives to pixels. Without clipping, coordinates outside the frame buffer would cause memory access violations, and processing invisible portions wastes computational resources.
